context: Tests whether the agent captures tone classification along the specific axes the skill specifies — formal/casual, masculine/feminine/neutral, and technical/lifestyle — in voice.md, rather than giving a vague tonal description. Also checks that the full voice.md required content is present.

checklist.name: Formal/casual axis stated
checklist.description: voice.md explicitly positions the brand on the formal-to-casual spectrum (e.g., 'casual', 'conversational', 'semi-formal', 'informal') — not just implied through examples

checklist.name: Gender-coded or neutral axis stated
checklist.description: voice.md explicitly describes the brand's tone as masculine-coded, feminine-coded, gender-neutral, or a specific variant (e.g., 'empowering feminine', 'neutral-inclusive') — not just implied

checklist.name: Technical/lifestyle axis stated
checklist.description: voice.md explicitly places the brand on the technical-to-lifestyle spectrum (e.g., 'lifestyle-forward', 'beauty-expert', 'accessible, not clinical') — not just implied

checklist.name: Tone summary section
checklist.description: voice.md has a dedicated tone summary section (a labeled heading or block) that consolidates the tonal description, not just scattered mentions

checklist.name: Vocabulary — words to use
checklist.description: voice.md contains a list or section of specific words or phrases the brand should use

checklist.name: Vocabulary — words to avoid
checklist.description: voice.md contains a list or section of specific words or phrases that are off-brand or should be avoided

checklist.name: 3–5 sample paragraphs
checklist.description: voice.md contains between 3 and 5 sample paragraphs written in the brand's voice

checklist.name: Opening lines
checklist.description: voice.md includes examples of common opening lines or how the brand typically begins communications

checklist.name: Closing lines
checklist.description: voice.md includes examples of common closing lines or how the brand typically ends communications
